records.ety: [OE. burne, borne, AS. burna; akin to OS. brunno spring, G. born, brunnen, OHG. prunno, Goth. brunna, Icel. brunnr, and perh. to Gr. <?/. The root is prob. that of burn, v., because the source of a stream seems to issue forth bubbling and boiling from the earth. Cf. Torrent, and see Burn, v.]

defs: A stream or rivulet; a burn.
quotes: My little boat can safely pass this perilous bourn.

defs: A bound; a boundary; a limit. Hence: Point aimed at; goal.
quotes: Where the land slopes to its watery bourn.
The undiscovered country, from whose bourn No traveler returns.
Sole bourn, sole wish, sole object of my song.
To make the doctrine . . . their intellectual bourne.
